fields.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #2 (HIGH). The push-retry path `src/rebar/_store/push.py` `_push_tickets_branch` (~L159-230) performs the same fetch + `git merge` + stash mutation that `src/rebar/_store/sync.py` `reconverge` (~L130-176) correctly guards with the write lock — but it runs **unlocked**, by documented design (push runs after the write lock is released). A concurrent writer that already passed its rebase guard can have its `git commit` fail against transient MERGE_HEAD/index activity from the push-retry merge, spuriously failing the user's write. In `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=async` mode this race is routine: a detached background process merges while the foreground batch keeps writing. Related (audit 3.2): the async detached spawn swallows its own `OSError` (`push_tickets_branch` async branch ~L108-138, `except OSError: pass` ~L136-137).

## What / files
- `src/rebar/_store/push.py`: keep the `fetch` (~L169) and `check_no_rebase_in_progress` (~L173) OUTSIDE the lock, then wrap ONLY the merge/stash mutation (~L181-228) in `with _lock.write_lock(base_path, timeout=<short>, attempts=1, dual_window=True):` mirroring sync.py (~L172). On `_lock.LockTimeout`, skip the merge and leave the push pending (best-effort) rather than racing — do NOT fail the user's write. `_lock` is already imported.
- Log the async-push detached spawn failure at WARNING instead of swallowing it silently (~L136-137).
- Regression test in `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` (use the `two_clones` fixture, ~L156): interleave a push-retry merge with a concurrent locked write and assert the write succeeds (no spurious StoreError) and no event is lost.

## Scope
In: push.py (lock around the merge portion only; spawn-failure logging); one concurrency regression test. Out: sync.py (already correct); changing push best-effort semantics (a push failure still never fails a write); stash-drop hardening (separate low finding).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The non-fast-forward reconcile merge in push.py runs under the store write lock; a lock timeout means the merge is skipped (push stays pending) rather than racing — verified by a test
- [ ] Async-push detached-spawn failure is logged at WARNING (no silent `except OSError: pass`)
- [ ] New test: a concurrent locked write during a push-retry merge — the write succeeds (no spurious StoreError) and no event is lost
- [ ] Push remains best-effort: no code path lets a push failure fail a write (existing push/sync tests stay green)
- [ ] `make test` green
